catalog_policy: Official-source facts and URLs with original RightOut notes only. No third-party broker-list records or prose.

brokers.name: California DROP
brokers.notes: Eligibility-specific government platform. RightOut records only a human-verified filing and later human-observed portal status. Government login and identity verification remain human-only. Broker processing begins 2026-08-01; the operational tracker uses the official 90-day processing window and 45-day broker access cycle. A portal status, including deleted, is not direct record-level deletion proof.

brokers.name: Google Results About You

sources.title: Find and remove personal contact info in Google Search results

brokers.notes: This lane concerns Google search-result visibility, not source-broker deletion. The public runner performs no account or provider action.

brokers.name: EU and EEA controller erasure reference
brokers.notes: Legal reference only, not a sendable recipient. Controller identity and domain must come from a future platform-owned verifier that the agent and controller content cannot alter.

brokers.name: FullEnrich
brokers.notes: FullEnrich's official policy lists erasure and says data subjects can exercise their rights by contacting support@fullenrich.com. RightOut sends contact email and country initially and never treats submission as erasure confirmation.

brokers.name: emetriq
brokers.notes: The official privacy notice directs data-subject rights to datenschutz@emetriq.com. RightOut initially discloses only the subject-controlled contact email and country. Because advertising data may be pseudonymous, the controller may need proportionate follow-up context; RightOut never invents or pre-emptively discloses extra identifiers.

brokers.name: EDAA YourOnlineChoices
brokers.notes: Pan-European one-stop preference control for participating online-advertising companies. It turns tailored advertising on or off through browser storage. It is not a universal data-broker registry and is not evidence of controller-wide erasure.

brokers.name: emetriq browser opt-out
brokers.notes: The official control stops further emetriq interest-profile collection in the current browser and relies on a retained opt-out cookie. Previously linked data can no longer be assigned to that browser ID and is described as automatically deleted after a short period; this is not controller-wide person-level proof.

brokers.name: Criteo EU rights portal
brokers.notes: Criteo distinguishes consent withdrawal, Article 17 erasure, Article 21 objection, and access. The official page routes erasure to a controller form, so RightOut keeps this human-only until a deterministic form contract is independently evidenced.

brokers.name: Zeotap EU rights portal
brokers.notes: Zeotap provides a website portal for Cookie-ID data and a mobile rights app for Ad-ID data. Because the relevant identifier must be obtained in the subject's browser or device, this remains human-only and no cross-identifier completeness is claimed.

brokers.name: Dealfront
brokers.notes: Dealfront's current data-subject notice says rights may be exercised by contacting privacy@dealfront.com. RightOut sends only contact email and country initially and requires human review of identification follow-up and controller response.

brokers.name: Snov.io
brokers.notes: Snov.io's current policy explicitly says a person may request deletion of all personal data by emailing help@snov.io. RightOut sends only contact email and country initially and never treats SMTP acceptance as deletion confirmation.

brokers.name: ID5 privacy and DSAR portal

sources.title: Website Privacy Policy and privacy portal routing

brokers.notes: ID5's current privacy page lists EEA and UK erasure rights and links its platform privacy choices to the ID5 Sync portal. Because the platform workflow is tied to browser or device context, it remains operator-controlled and no cross-device completeness is claimed.

brokers.name: Kaspr
brokers.notes: Kaspr's official policy explicitly says EU/EEA data subjects may exercise deletion and erasure rights by writing to privacy@kaspr.io. RightOut sends only contact email and country initially; profile matching and identity follow-up remain human-reviewed.

brokers.name: StackAdapt
brokers.notes: StackAdapt's current policy explicitly permits privacy-rights requests at privacy@stackadapt.com. SMTP acceptance remains only a submitted controller request.

brokers.name: Bombora
brokers.notes: Bombora's current policy directs data-subject requests to dsars@bombora.com and lists deletion rights. No company-level aggregation is reported as individual erasure without a human-reviewed controller response.

brokers.name: Seedtag
brokers.notes: Seedtag's official advertising privacy policy lists erasure and directs rights requests to gdpr@seedtag.com. RightOut makes no claim beyond controller receipt and later reviewed outcome.

brokers.name: Audiencerate
brokers.notes: Audiencerate's official policy lists erasure and privacy@audiencerate.com as the rights channel. Extra identifiers remain proportionate follow-up only.

brokers.name: Lead411
brokers.notes: Lead411's current policy lists European deletion rights and permits requests through support@lead411.io. RightOut sends name, subject-controlled email, and country initially and keeps verification and outcome human-reviewed.

brokers.name: Surfe
brokers.notes: Surfe's current policy covers individuals whose business-contact data it collects and sells, lists erasure, and says these rights can be exercised through dpo@surfe.com. RightOut never treats submission as erasure confirmation.

brokers.name: GumGum
brokers.notes: GumGum's current policy gives EEA users erasure rights through dataprivacy@gumgum.com. Pseudonymous data may require follow-up and is not claimed removed from submission alone.

brokers.name: Smaato
brokers.notes: Smaato's current policy offers manual erasure instructions through privacy@smaato.com. Device identifiers and controller-versus-processor scope remain follow-up questions, not inferred facts.

brokers.name: Teads
brokers.notes: Teads' current user policy says privacy rights may be exercised by emailing privacy@teads.com. Identity and residency confirmation remain controller follow-up.

brokers.name: MiQ
brokers.notes: MiQ's official privacy page explicitly directs deletion requests to privacy@miqdigital.com. RightOut treats every response and claimed exception as human-reviewed evidence.

brokers.name: 6sense
brokers.notes: 6sense's current policy covers Business Profile Information in its Business Database, lists EEA deletion rights, and permits privacy-rights requests by email to privacy@6sense.com. Name, subject-controlled email, and country are sent initially; any verification remains human-reviewed.

brokers.name: Cognism
brokers.notes: Cognism's current policy covers its business-contact platform, lists deletion rights, and permits requests through privacy@cognism.com. Name is included because the official process locates professional profiles rather than device-only data.

brokers.name: Cognism UK rights
brokers.notes: Cognism's version 12 January 2026 policy expressly covers people based in the EU, EEA, and UK, identifies Cognism Limited in England, lists erasure and objection rights, permits requests through privacy@cognism.com, allows proportionate identity follow-up, and names the ICO. This UK route is a separate contract and never reuses the EU template, process class, eligibility gate, or deadline.

brokers.name: Lusha
brokers.notes: Lusha's official privacy documentation says data subjects may request permanent deletion directly from privacy@lusha.com. Name and subject-controlled email are the minimum initial professional-profile identifiers.

brokers.name: Amplemarket
brokers.notes: Amplemarket's official California statement says access, portability, deletion, and sale opt-out requests may be submitted by email to privacy@amplemarket.com. RightOut sends minimum initial identifiers; any additional verification remains human-reviewed.

brokers.name: SalesIntel
brokers.notes: SalesIntel's official policy describes California deletion and opt-out rights and lists support@salesintel.io. DROP remains a separate human-only government-platform route.

brokers.name: LeadIQ
brokers.notes: LeadIQ's official policy provides privacy@leadiq.com for directory removal and suppression. RightOut records only the request until a reviewed controller outcome exists.

brokers.name: Wiza
brokers.notes: Wiza's official policy publishes optout@wiza.com for US state deletion and opt-out requests. The cross-domain recipient is explicitly allowlisted from the same official policy.

brokers.name: SignalHire
brokers.notes: SignalHire's policy says identity evidence may be requested. RightOut sends no attachment or identity document; any such request is a human-only follow-up and may be declined or minimized.

brokers.name: Hunter
brokers.notes: Hunter's official policy describes US state deletion and opt-out rights and publishes privacy@hunter.io. The email lane never implies that indexed third-party sources were also deleted.

brokers.name: Seamless.AI
brokers.notes: Seamless.AI's official policy states that applicable users may request deletion through its Privacy Request Center or by contacting the published privacy address. RightOut uses the explicit email option, minimum initial identifiers, and never treats submission as deletion confirmation.

brokers.name: ContactOut
brokers.notes: ContactOut's official data-subject page covers profile removal and publishes support@contactout.com. Verification and any additional evidence remain human-reviewed.

brokers.name: TruePeopleSearch
brokers.notes: Discovery remains Brave-index-only. A separate native approval can directly read only an encrypted exact candidate URL after operator terms review; index absence never proves removal, redirects/CAPTCHA fail closed, and any absence result is limited to the known listing set. Removal submission remains human-only.

brokers.name: BeenVerified
brokers.notes: Brave-only discovery remains read-only. A separate allow-once removal tool may send one minimum-disclosure deletion and opt-out email to the official privacy address for an attested California subject. SMTP acceptance is submitted, never confirmed_removed. Separate approvals cover inbox verification, confirmation-link opening, and an exact-URL direct recheck after operator terms review; confirmed_removed is scoped to the known listing set.

brokers.name: Intelius
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only. The separately approved browser-form lane sends only the contact email to initiate the official suppression flow; email verification and subject selection remain before any removal can be claimed.

brokers.name: US Search
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only. The ownership relationship is sourced independently from the official California registry.

brokers.name: Nuwber
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only; absence remains inconclusive.

brokers.name: Whitepages
brokers.notes: Official California registry evidence identifies the privacy entry point; automated publisher access remains disabled.

brokers.name: USPeopleSearch
brokers.notes: The request page is cataloged for human planning only; live discovery remains Brave-only.

brokers.name: CheckPeople
brokers.notes: The request entry point is not automated until its form contract and terms are independently verified.

brokers.name: FreePeopleSearch.com
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only; no publisher page is fetched.

brokers.name: PeopleWhiz
brokers.notes: The removal entry point remains human-planned until a safe form contract exists.

brokers.name: PeopleFinders
brokers.notes: Discovery uses the Brave index only; no removal form is automated in this lane.

brokers.name: FastPeopleSearch
brokers.notes: The 2025 California registry identifies the ownership cluster and states that a request on one listed site applies across the listed sites; RightOut still requires later per-site verification.

brokers.name: USA People Search
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in an independently evidenced ownership cluster; publisher pages are not fetched.

brokers.name: Advanced Background Checks
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: People Search Now
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: USPhoneBook
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: SearchPeopleFree
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: SmartBackgroundChecks
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: CyberBackgroundChecks
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: FastBackgroundCheck
brokers.notes: Child discovery lane in the official-registry ownership cluster.

brokers.name: ThatsThem
brokers.notes: The official registry identifies Spokeo ownership but explicitly describes a separate opt-out for ThatsThem.

brokers.name: Spokeo
brokers.notes: Automated live scanning is disabled. Spokeo's published consumer terms prohibit automated queries, scraping, crawling, data mining, robots, spiders, and other automatic devices; its robots policy disallows search/profile paths. Any review is manual and out of the plugin live tool.
